title: Mythology of Evangelion WTF
wikitext:
[]: Mythology of Evangelion: WTF?             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Sat Oct 22, 2005 10:22 am I finally obtained Evangelion Platinum Edition vol 07 yesterday, and after watching the feature decided to view the second part of the â€œMythology of Evangelionâ€ that was first seen in vol 05. I enjoyed the interpretation that they had put on the series (although I doubt it is entirely correct) in the irst part and was looking forward to seeing what they would cover in the second part. Ok so tell me: What the hell? The entire thing is riddled with these flashes of the Seele â€˜sound onlyâ€™ monoliths, and after only 11 mins, the entire things ends abruptly with a â€˜top secretâ€™ message just before Sean McCoy started to talk about Seeleâ€™s goals. So what the hell was this? Was it a lame joke by ADV, mainly to cover the fact that they couldnâ€™t fill another 20 mins, or is there something else after this screen, and you have to press some button in order to access it? If the former is the correct answer, Iâ€™ll be rather pissed. The extraâ€™s they provided with Platinum were pathetic to begin with, how can they justify making the second part of one of there only interesting extras a third of the length of the first? Anyway, Iâ€™ve never had a problem with ADV up until this point, but this really pissed me off. Thoughts/Answers?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Oct 22, 2005 8:28 pm It's all shit. You should be pissed; the extras do suck. If they hadn't done that "Sound Only" thing, you still wouldn't have learned anything useful. Now let's all forget our troubles over a big bowl of strawberry ice cream.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Sat Oct 22, 2005 8:37 pm Oh my god. Were those the one where they were all talking about how all Evangelion was about was growing up?

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Sun Oct 23, 2005 1:42 am Yeah, but I think they were mainly basing that on the fact that Shamshell looks like a massive penis.

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Sun Oct 23, 2005 7:20 am ThroneofDravaris wrote: Yeah, but I think they were mainly basing that on the fact that Shamshell looks like a massive penis.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Sun Oct 23, 2005 12:54 pm sachiel wrote: ThroneofDravaris wrote: Yeah, but I think they were mainly basing that on the fact that Shamshell looks like a massive penis.

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Mon Oct 24, 2005 2:49 am Yeah, but you can't fill 30 mins with a single penis joke.

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Mon Oct 24, 2005 7:43 pm I know someone who can...

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Oct 25, 2005 7:03 pm ThroneofDravaris wrote: Yeah, but you can't fill 30 mins with a single penis joke.

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Wed Oct 26, 2005 4:26 am ...I sense some dissatisfaction with ADV here.... As far as I've seen, they're one of the only decent dubbing companies. Them and Pioneer.

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Wed Oct 26, 2005 5:01 am Yes. Pioneer is the best. Sound systems + a great Anime series = Very good company. I guess... I must also, pay them back a few DVD series though...

[]:             Quote Post by Phoenix Feet » Thu Oct 27, 2005 5:19 pm Seriously, how many of the people here would have ever heard of Eva if it hadn't been for ADV? Maybe they do things that piss you off, but a bit of grattitude wouldn't hurt.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Oct 27, 2005 6:20 pm Fuck them. If they hadn't done it, someone else would have, and if not, many would have downloaded it eventually. That's like saying Wal-Mart deserves gratitude for stocking Kit Kat bars, Kleenex Ass-Wipes, Ketchup, and Keith Urban CDs. ADV have allowed themselves to become a farce of a professional company; their entire front speaks for itself. Any hatred bred against them is of their own creation, including mine. Their success is but acid rain on the plains of their potential. They can be summed up in one word: arrogance. And I stand by my penis comment. I can assure you that ADV would stand by it, too.

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Thu Oct 27, 2005 7:50 pm Phoenix Feet wrote: Seriously, how many of the people here would have ever heard of Eva if it hadn't been for ADV? Maybe they do things that piss you off, but a bit of grattitude wouldn't hurt.

[]:             Quote Post by Phoenix Feet » Thu Oct 27, 2005 8:06 pm superdoughboy4 wrote: Think about it though... The people that have watched the whole EVA series, dubbed, in ADV dub especially, will not know the story for real. So, it's like the audience knowing EVA, but not the true EVA, which would just bring upon lame questions.

[]:             Quote Post by Phoenix Feet » Thu Oct 27, 2005 8:11 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: Fuck them. If they hadn't done it, someone else would have, and if not, many would have downloaded it eventually. That's like saying Wal-Mart deserves gratitude for stocking Kit Kat bars, Kleenex Ass-Wipes, Ketchup, and Keith Urban CDs. ADV have allowed themselves to become a farce of a professional company; their entire front speaks for itself. Any hatred bred against them is of their own creation, including mine. Their success is but acid rain on the plains of their potential. They can be summed up in one word: arrogance. And I stand by my penis comment. I can assure you that ADV would stand by it, too.

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Thu Oct 27, 2005 8:12 pm But lazy interpretations were implemented in the dubs, which would lead-off the main story, and plot it somewhere near the story instead.

[]:             Quote Post by Phoenix Feet » Thu Oct 27, 2005 8:16 pm superdoughboy4 wrote: But lazy interpretations were implemented in the dubs, which would lead-off the main story, and plot it somewhere near the story instead.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Oct 28, 2005 12:02 am Phoenix Feet wrote: The fact that someone else could have done it is no reason not to feel grattitude. Mum makes you lunch; no need to thank her, because Dad would have done it if she hadn't. I don't know what ADV has done to generate that much hate in you, but I'm not in a position to judge. Over here, everything goes through Madman, and they've been good so far.

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Fri Oct 28, 2005 7:25 am superdoughboy4 wrote: Think about it though... The people that have watched the whole EVA series, dubbed, in ADV dub especially, will not know the story for real. So, it's like the audience knowing EVA, but not the true EVA, which would just bring upon lame questions.
